worktree_state: clean on branch main — all changes committed and pushed. Branch 'next' deleted locally and from remote. slack-bot/cloud-init/controller.yml remains locally dirty with live secrets; intentionally never committed.
current_focus: Debugging and extending the Slack bot: fixed /do-restore droplet creation (size slug, duplicate action_id), replaced 5-button pickers with static_select dropdowns showing all snapshots, added /do-help command, added --auto-destroy flag and interactive prompt to do-restore.sh. All changes merged to main.
exact_next_step: Apply the updated Slack app manifest to register /do-help: open https://api.slack.com/apps → DO Snap Bot → App Manifest → YAML tab → paste contents of slack-bot/manifest.yml → Save Changes.

blockers.description: slack-bot/cloud-init/controller.yml contains a live 1Password service account token and SSH public key — must never be staged or committed. Always skip in git add.
/do-help slash command added to manifest.yml this session but not yet re-applied at api.slack.com. Until the manifest is updated, /do-help will return 'dispatch_failed' in Slack.

open_tasks: Apply updated manifest.yml at api.slack.com to register /do-help (see exact_next_step above).
Add auto-destroy support to the Slack bot's /do-restore command (analog of the --auto-destroy flag added to do-restore.sh this session) — not yet implemented in bot.py.
Live-test /do-restore end-to-end with a real snapshot to confirm size-slug auto-selection and ssh-key attachment work correctly in production.
Evaluate Oracle Cloud Always Free as zero-cost controller droplet replacement (see docs/slack-integration-options.md).
Optionally configure scheduled snapshots and retention policy in .env.op on the controller (see docs/next-steps.md Part 4).
completed_this_session: Fixed /do-restore 'Droplet creation failed': root cause was hardcoded size_slug 's-1vcpu-1gb' (25 GB) rejected by DO when snapshot min_disk_size > 25 GB. Now auto-selects smallest satisfying size from a stepped map (s-1vcpu-1gb → s-8vcpu-16gb).
Added --ssh-keys arg to the restore droplet create call (was missing; _droplet_create_job already had it).

Fixed Block Kit 'invalid_blocks' error in snapshot selection: all 5 buttons shared action_id 'selection_pick'; Slack rejects duplicate action_ids in one actions block. Replaced button layout with a single static_select dropdown (supports up to 100 options).
Removed 5-snapshot cap from /do-restore, /do-snapshot-delete, and /do-droplet-create — all pickers now show every available snapshot sorted newest-first.
Added /do-help command: posts full grouped command reference in-channel; registered in manifest.yml.

Added --auto-destroy DURATION flag to do-restore.sh: validates <N><m|h|d> at parse time, shows scheduled destroy time in confirm panel, spawns disowned background subshell after successful create, prints cancel PID.
Added interactive auto-destroy prompt to do-restore.sh when flag is omitted: Y/N confirm → preset picker (30m/1h/2h/4h/8h/12h/24h/2d/7d/custom) → loops on custom input until valid.
Updated README.md: What's included, Quick start (bash + Slack full command list), Typical workflow, manifest update note.
All changes committed and pushed; branch 'next' merged to main and deleted.
